assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 reads the Last-Updated timestamp from the security-matrix.md HTML comment
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md line 21 explicitly reports the extracted timestamp: 'Last-Updated timestamp: 2026-06-28T10:00:00Z'. This ISO 8601 value is consistent with having been parsed from an HTML comment in the security-matrix.md file.

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 determines the matrix is within the 14-day threshold and proceeds without displaying a staleness warning
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md lines 22-27: 'Age: 11 days (from 2026-06-28 to 2026-07-09)', 'Threshold: 14 days', and 'Result: FRESH -- Proceed. The security matrix was last updated 11 days ago, which is within the 14-day staleness threshold. No refresh is required.' No staleness warning is displayed.

assertion_results.text: No user prompt or options are presented for a fresh matrix — the check is silent on success
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md line 33 states: 'Proceed with triage using the current matrix data. No user intervention required.' The output contains no interactive prompt, no options menu, and no request for user input. The decision to proceed is automatic.

assertion_results.text: The triage continues to Step 0.5 and beyond without interruption from the staleness check
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md contains the full output of Step 1 (Data Extraction), which is beyond Step 0.5. The presence of this file with complete CVE data extraction (CVE-2026-31812, stream scope resolution, ecosystem detection, etc.) demonstrates that the triage continued past the staleness check without interruption.
